The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. (1962). ''Sovereign and Subject''. Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':

{{lang|ja|源 実朝}} | image = File:Painting of Minamoto no Sanetomo by Yashima Gakutei, 1825.jpg | caption = Painting of Minamoto no Sanetomo by [[:en:Yashima Gakutei|Yashima Gakutei]]. [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] circa 1825. | office = ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' | term_start = 1203 | term_end = 1219 | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Tsuchimikado|Tsuchimikado]]
[[:en:Emperor Juntoku|Juntoku]] | 1blankname = ''[[:en:Shikken|Shikken]]'' | 1namedata = [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]]
[[:en:Hōjō Yoshitoki|Hōjō Yoshitoki]] | predecessor = [[Minamoto no Yoriie]] | successor = [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]] | birth_date = {{birth date|1192|9|17}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1219|2|13|1192|9|17}} | death_place = | spouse = Bomon Nobuko | children = | father = [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | mother = [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] | signature = Minamoto no Sanetomo kao.jpg | nationality = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | branch = [[Minamoto clan]] }} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Sanetomo'''|源 実朝||September 12, 1192 – February 13, 1219, r. 1203–1219}} was the third ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. He was the second son of the Kamakura shogunate founder, [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. His mother was [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and his older brother was the second Kamakura shogun [[Minamoto no Yoriie]]. His childhood name was {{nihongo|'''Senman'''|千万}}. He was the last head of the [[Minamoto clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was {{nihongo|'''Daijijiden seini'i goshoko jingi'''|大慈寺殿正二位丞相公神儀}}. He was an accomplished ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poet. == Early life == [[File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|thumb|220px|Sanetomo in court clothing, painting by the priest Goshin.]] {{more citations needed|section|date=March 2018}} After the death of Yoritomo in 1199, Sanetomo's maternal grandfather [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]] usurped the political and military power of the Shogunate, relegating the position and title of ''[[:en:Sei-i Taishōgun|Sei-i Taishōgun]]'', or ''shōgun'', to a figurehead. Through hereditary succession, Sanetomo's older brother Yoriie became ''Sei-i Taishōgun'' in 1202, only to be stripped of the title a year later and put under house arrest for plotting against the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]].{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} This was presumably to keep the ''shōgun'' a child and thus needing a regent (''shikken'') to make decisions in his place. Shortly thereafter, in 1203, Sanetomo became head of the Minamoto clan and was appointed ''Sei-i Taishōgun''. A year later, Yoriie was assassinated by the Hōjō. Sanetomo was never more than a puppet for his mother [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and maternal uncle Hōjō Yoshitoki, who used him as a pawn in their war with their father Tokimasa; Tokimasa tried to depose his grandson a number of times, beginning in 1205, causing Sanetomo to fear for his life thereafter.{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === ''Waka'' poet === Sanetomo, understanding his own powerlessness and not wanting to meet the same fate as his elder brother, put his time and energy into writing [[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka poetry]] and gaining posts within the powerless but honorary imperial court. Sanetomo was a talented poet, writing over 700 poems between the age 17 and 22 while he was tutored by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}}. He published his private waka collection ''Kinkai Wakashū'', even having one of his [[waka (poetry)#Tanka|tanka]] included in the anthology ''Ogura [[:en:Hyakunin Isshu|Hyakunin Isshu]]'' ("100 Poems by 100 Poets"), a collection of Japanese poems of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and early Kamakura periods. Sanetomo reached the third-highest post of the imperial court, ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' (Minister of the Right or "vice-premier") in 1218.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Sanetomo" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', pp. 633–634|page=633}} Eventually, Sanetomo lapsed into inactivity and despair, plagued by fear of assassination and tormented by his chronic [[:en:alcoholism|alcoholism]] (an addiction which Priest [[:en:Eisai|Eisai]] once tried to break by replacing alcohol with [[:en:Tea|Tea]]).{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === Death === [[File:Tsurugaoka.jpg|thumb|Grand stairway at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in Kamakura – the scene of Sanetomo's assassination]] [[File:KamakuraHachimanguTree.jpg|thumb|Shinto symbolism encompasses the girth of the ancient ginkgo tree at the foot of the stairs leading to the upper level of Kamakura's great Hachiman Shrine. The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. (1962). ''Sovereign and Subject''. Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':
Minamoto no Sanetomo | before=[[Minamoto no Yoriie]] | after=[[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]] | years=1203–1219}} {{s-end}} {{Shoguns}} {{Authority control}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1219 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:13th-century shōguns]] [[Category:Kamakura shōguns]] [[Category:Minamoto clan]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men]] [[Category:Imperial House of Japan]] [[Category:Samurai]] [[Category:People of Kamakura-period Japan]] [[Category:Assassinated Japanese people]] [[Category:Hyakunin Isshu poets]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese calligraphers]] }} [[en:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ميناموتو وسانيتومو]] [[be:Мінамота-но Санэтома]] [[ca:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[de:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[es:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[eu:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[fa:میناموتو نو سانه‌تومو]] [[fr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[it:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[ja:源実朝]] [[ko:미나모토노 사네토모]] [[la:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[mr:मिनामोटो नो सानेतोमो]] [[nl:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[pl:Sanetomo Minamoto]] [[pt:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[ru:Минамото-но Санэтомо]] [[sv:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[th:มินาโมโตะ โนะ ซาเนโตโมะ]] [[tr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[uk:Мінамото но Санетомо]] [[vi:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[zh:源實朝]] [[zh-classical:源實朝]] [[zh-min-nan:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] {{wikidata link|Q174249}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

His mother was [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and his older brother was the second Kamakura shogun [[Minamoto no Yoriie]]. His childhood name was {{nihongo|'''Senman'''|千万}}. He was an accomplished ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poet. == Early life == [[File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|thumb|220px|Sanetomo in court clothing, painting by the priest Goshin.]] {{more citations needed|section|date=March 2018}} After the death of Yoritomo in 1199, Sanetomo's maternal grandfather [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]] usurped the political and military power of the Shogunate, relegating the position and title of ''[[:en:Sei-i Taishōgun|Sei-i Taishōgun]]'', or ''shōgun'', to a figurehead. Through hereditary succession, Sanetomo's older brother Yoriie became ''Sei-i Taishōgun'' in 1202, only to be stripped of the title a year later and put under house arrest for plotting against the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]].{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} This was presumably to keep the ''shōgun'' a child and thus needing a regent (''shikken'') to make decisions in his place. Shortly thereafter, in 1203, Sanetomo became head of the Minamoto clan and was appointed ''Sei-i Taishōgun''. A year later, Yoriie was assassinated by the Hōjō. Sanetomo was never more than a puppet for his mother [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and maternal uncle Hōjō Yoshitoki, who used him as a pawn in their war with their father Tokimasa; Tokimasa tried to depose his grandson a number of times, beginning in 1205, causing Sanetomo to fear for his life thereafter.{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === ''Waka'' poet === Sanetomo, understanding his own powerlessness and not wanting to meet the same fate as his elder brother, put his time and energy into writing [[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka poetry]] and gaining posts within the powerless but honorary imperial court. Sanetomo was a talented poet, writing over 700 poems between the age 17 and 22 while he was tutored by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}}. He published his private waka collection ''Kinkai Wakashū'', even having one of his [[waka (poetry)#Tanka|tanka]] included in the anthology ''Ogura [[:en:Hyakunin Isshu|Hyakunin Isshu]]'' ("100 Poems by 100 Poets"), a collection of Japanese poems of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and early Kamakura periods. Sanetomo reached the third-highest post of the imperial court, ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' (Minister of the Right or "vice-premier") in 1218.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Sanetomo" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', pp. 633–634|page=633}} Eventually, Sanetomo lapsed into inactivity and despair, plagued by fear of assassination and tormented by his chronic [[:en:alcoholism|alcoholism]] (an addiction which Priest [[:en:Eisai|Eisai]] once tried to break by replacing alcohol with [[:en:Tea|Tea]]).{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === Death === [[File:Tsurugaoka.jpg|thumb|Grand stairway at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in Kamakura – the scene of Sanetomo's assassination]] [[File:KamakuraHachimanguTree.jpg|thumb|Shinto symbolism encompasses the girth of the ancient ginkgo tree at the foot of the stairs leading to the upper level of Kamakura's great Hachiman Shrine. The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. He was the second son of the Kamakura shogunate founder, [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. He was an accomplished ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poet. == Early life == [[File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|thumb|220px|Sanetomo in court clothing, painting by the priest Goshin.]] {{more citations needed|section|date=March 2018}} After the death of Yoritomo in 1199, Sanetomo's maternal grandfather [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]] usurped the political and military power of the Shogunate, relegating the position and title of ''[[:en:Sei-i Taishōgun|Sei-i Taishōgun]]'', or ''shōgun'', to a figurehead. He published his private waka collection ''Kinkai Wakashū'', even having one of his [[waka (poetry)#Tanka|tanka]] included in the anthology ''Ogura [[:en:Hyakunin Isshu|Hyakunin Isshu]]'' ("100 Poems by 100 Poets"), a collection of Japanese poems of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and early Kamakura periods. [[arz:ميناموتو وسانيتومو]][[be:Мінамота-но Санэтома]][[ca:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[de:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[en:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[es:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[eu:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[fa:میناموتو نو سانه‌تومو]][[fr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[it:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[ja:源実朝]][[ko:미나모토노 사네토모]][[la:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[mr:मिनामोटो नो सानेतोमो]][[nl:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[pl:Sanetomo Minamoto]][[pt:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[ru:Минамото-но Санэтомо]][[sv:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[th:มินาโมโตะ โนะ ซาเนโตโมะ]][[tr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[uk:Мінамото но Санетомо]][[vi:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[zh-classical:源實朝]][[zh-min-nan:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[zh:源實朝]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1192|9|17}}|death_date={{death date|1219|2|13}}|birth_place={{ill|Nagoe|WD=Q11415640}}|death_place={{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū|WD=Q701403}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|calligrapher|WD=Q3303330}}; {{ill|poet|WD=Q49757}}; {{ill|writer|WD=Q36180}}|title={{ill|shogun|WD=Q131767}}|spouse={{ill|Bōmon Nobuko|WD=Q498600}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800}}|mother={{ill|Hōjō Masako|WD=Q463961}}|relatives={{ill|Jōgyō|WD=Q11634528}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|WD=Q431052}}; {{ill|Sanman|WD=Q11355364}}; {{ill|Ōhime|WD=Q11434217}}|family={{ill|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652}}|notable_works={{ill|Kinkai Wakashū|WD=Q11647307}}|native_name=源実朝|resting_place={{ill|Jufuku-ji|WD=Q167087}}; {{ill|Shōchōju-in|WD=Q17219243}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q174249]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Sanetomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Sanetomo
{{lang|ja|源 実朝}} | image = File:Painting of Minamoto no Sanetomo by Yashima Gakutei, 1825.jpg | caption = Painting of Minamoto no Sanetomo by [[:en:Yashima Gakutei|Yashima Gakutei]]. [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] circa 1825. | office = ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' | term_start = 1203 | term_end = 1219 | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Tsuchimikado|Tsuchimikado]]
[[:en:Emperor Juntoku|Juntoku]] | 1blankname = ''[[:en:Shikken|Shikken]]'' | 1namedata = [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]]
[[:en:Hōjō Yoshitoki|Hōjō Yoshitoki]] | predecessor = [[Minamoto no Yoriie]] | successor = [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]] | birth_date = {{birth date|1192|9|17}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1219|2|13|1192|9|17}} | death_place = | spouse = Bomon Nobuko | children = | father = [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | mother = [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] | signature = Minamoto no Sanetomo kao.jpg | nationality = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | branch = [[Minamoto clan]] }} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Sanetomo'''|源 実朝||September 12, 1192 – February 13, 1219, r. 1203–1219}} was the third ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. He was the second son of the Kamakura shogunate founder, [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. His mother was [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and his older brother was the second Kamakura shogun [[Minamoto no Yoriie]]. His childhood name was {{nihongo|'''Senman'''|千万}}. He was the last head of the [[Minamoto clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was {{nihongo|'''Daijijiden seini'i goshoko jingi'''|大慈寺殿正二位丞相公神儀}}. He was an accomplished ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poet. == Early life == [[File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|thumb|220px|Sanetomo in court clothing, painting by the priest Goshin.]] {{more citations needed|section|date=March 2018}} After the death of Yoritomo in 1199, Sanetomo's maternal grandfather [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]] usurped the political and military power of the Shogunate, relegating the position and title of ''[[:en:Sei-i Taishōgun|Sei-i Taishōgun]]'', or ''shōgun'', to a figurehead. Through hereditary succession, Sanetomo's older brother Yoriie became ''Sei-i Taishōgun'' in 1202, only to be stripped of the title a year later and put under house arrest for plotting against the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]].{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} This was presumably to keep the ''shōgun'' a child and thus needing a regent (''shikken'') to make decisions in his place. Shortly thereafter, in 1203, Sanetomo became head of the Minamoto clan and was appointed ''Sei-i Taishōgun''. A year later, Yoriie was assassinated by the Hōjō. Sanetomo was never more than a puppet for his mother [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and maternal uncle Hōjō Yoshitoki, who used him as a pawn in their war with their father Tokimasa; Tokimasa tried to depose his grandson a number of times, beginning in 1205, causing Sanetomo to fear for his life thereafter.{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === ''Waka'' poet === Sanetomo, understanding his own powerlessness and not wanting to meet the same fate as his elder brother, put his time and energy into writing [[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka poetry]] and gaining posts within the powerless but honorary imperial court. Sanetomo was a talented poet, writing over 700 poems between the age 17 and 22 while he was tutored by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}}. He published his private waka collection ''Kinkai Wakashū'', even having one of his [[waka (poetry)#Tanka|tanka]] included in the anthology ''Ogura [[:en:Hyakunin Isshu|Hyakunin Isshu]]'' ("100 Poems by 100 Poets"), a collection of Japanese poems of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and early Kamakura periods. Sanetomo reached the third-highest post of the imperial court, ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' (Minister of the Right or "vice-premier") in 1218.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Sanetomo" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', pp. 633–634|page=633}} Eventually, Sanetomo lapsed into inactivity and despair, plagued by fear of assassination and tormented by his chronic [[:en:alcoholism|alcoholism]] (an addiction which Priest [[:en:Eisai|Eisai]] once tried to break by replacing alcohol with [[:en:Tea|Tea]]).{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === Death === [[File:Tsurugaoka.jpg|thumb|Grand stairway at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in Kamakura – the scene of Sanetomo's assassination]] [[File:KamakuraHachimanguTree.jpg|thumb|Shinto symbolism encompasses the girth of the ancient ginkgo tree at the foot of the stairs leading to the upper level of Kamakura's great Hachiman Shrine. The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. (1962). ''Sovereign and Subject''. Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':

Sanetomo reached the third-highest post of the imperial court, ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' (Minister of the Right or "vice-premier") in 1218.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Sanetomo" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', pp. 633–634|page=633}} Eventually, Sanetomo lapsed into inactivity and despair, plagued by fear of assassination and tormented by his chronic [[:en:alcoholism|alcoholism]] (an addiction which Priest [[:en:Eisai|Eisai]] once tried to break by replacing alcohol with [[:en:Tea|Tea]]).{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === Death === [[File:Tsurugaoka.jpg|thumb|Grand stairway at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in Kamakura – the scene of Sanetomo's assassination]] [[File:KamakuraHachimanguTree.jpg|thumb|Shinto symbolism encompasses the girth of the ancient ginkgo tree at the foot of the stairs leading to the upper level of Kamakura's great Hachiman Shrine. The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. (1962). ''Sovereign and Subject''. Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':

[[arz:ميناموتو وسانيتومو]][[be:Мінамота-но Санэтома]][[ca:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[de:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[en:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[es:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[eu:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[fa:میناموتو نو سانه‌تومو]][[fr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[it:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[ja:源実朝]][[ko:미나모토노 사네토모]][[la:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[mr:मिनामोटो नो सानेतोमो]][[nl:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[pl:Sanetomo Minamoto]][[pt:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[ru:Минамото-но Санэтомо]][[sv:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[th:มินาโมโตะ โนะ ซาเนโตโมะ]][[tr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[uk:Мінамото но Санетомо]][[vi:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[zh-classical:源實朝]][[zh-min-nan:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[zh:源實朝]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1192|9|17}}|death_date={{death date|1219|2|13}}|birth_place={{ill|Nagoe|WD=Q11415640}}|death_place={{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū|WD=Q701403}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|calligrapher|WD=Q3303330}}; {{ill|poet|WD=Q49757}}; {{ill|writer|WD=Q36180}}|title={{ill|shogun|WD=Q131767}}|spouse={{ill|Bōmon Nobuko|WD=Q498600}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800}}|mother={{ill|Hōjō Masako|WD=Q463961}}|relatives={{ill|Jōgyō|WD=Q11634528}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|WD=Q431052}}; {{ill|Sanman|WD=Q11355364}}; {{ill|Ōhime|WD=Q11434217}}|family={{ill|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652}}|notable_works={{ill|Kinkai Wakashū|WD=Q11647307}}|native_name=源実朝|resting_place={{ill|Jufuku-ji|WD=Q167087}}; {{ill|Shōchōju-in|WD=Q17219243}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q174249]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Sanetomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Sanetomo
{{lang|ja|源 実朝}} | image = File:Painting of Minamoto no Sanetomo by Yashima Gakutei, 1825.jpg | caption = Painting of Minamoto no Sanetomo by [[:en:Yashima Gakutei|Yashima Gakutei]]. [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] circa 1825. | office = ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' | term_start = 1203 | term_end = 1219 | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Tsuchimikado|Tsuchimikado]]
[[:en:Emperor Juntoku|Juntoku]] | 1blankname = ''[[:en:Shikken|Shikken]]'' | 1namedata = [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]]
[[:en:Hōjō Yoshitoki|Hōjō Yoshitoki]] | predecessor = [[Minamoto no Yoriie]] | successor = [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]] | birth_date = {{birth date|1192|9|17}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1219|2|13|1192|9|17}} | death_place = | spouse = Bomon Nobuko | children = | father = [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | mother = [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] | signature = Minamoto no Sanetomo kao.jpg | nationality = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | branch = [[Minamoto clan]] }} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Sanetomo'''|源 実朝||September 12, 1192 – February 13, 1219, r. 1203–1219}} was the third ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. He was the second son of the Kamakura shogunate founder, [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. His mother was [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and his older brother was the second Kamakura shogun [[Minamoto no Yoriie]]. His childhood name was {{nihongo|'''Senman'''|千万}}. He was the last head of the [[Minamoto clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was {{nihongo|'''Daijijiden seini'i goshoko jingi'''|大慈寺殿正二位丞相公神儀}}. He was an accomplished ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poet. == Early life == [[File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|thumb|220px|Sanetomo in court clothing, painting by the priest Goshin.]] {{more citations needed|section|date=March 2018}} After the death of Yoritomo in 1199, Sanetomo's maternal grandfather [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]] usurped the political and military power of the Shogunate, relegating the position and title of ''[[:en:Sei-i Taishōgun|Sei-i Taishōgun]]'', or ''shōgun'', to a figurehead. Through hereditary succession, Sanetomo's older brother Yoriie became ''Sei-i Taishōgun'' in 1202, only to be stripped of the title a year later and put under house arrest for plotting against the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]].{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} This was presumably to keep the ''shōgun'' a child and thus needing a regent (''shikken'') to make decisions in his place. Shortly thereafter, in 1203, Sanetomo became head of the Minamoto clan and was appointed ''Sei-i Taishōgun''. A year later, Yoriie was assassinated by the Hōjō. Sanetomo was never more than a puppet for his mother [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and maternal uncle Hōjō Yoshitoki, who used him as a pawn in their war with their father Tokimasa; Tokimasa tried to depose his grandson a number of times, beginning in 1205, causing Sanetomo to fear for his life thereafter.{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === ''Waka'' poet === Sanetomo, understanding his own powerlessness and not wanting to meet the same fate as his elder brother, put his time and energy into writing [[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka poetry]] and gaining posts within the powerless but honorary imperial court. Sanetomo was a talented poet, writing over 700 poems between the age 17 and 22 while he was tutored by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}}. He published his private waka collection ''Kinkai Wakashū'', even having one of his [[waka (poetry)#Tanka|tanka]] included in the anthology ''Ogura [[:en:Hyakunin Isshu|Hyakunin Isshu]]'' ("100 Poems by 100 Poets"), a collection of Japanese poems of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and early Kamakura periods. Sanetomo reached the third-highest post of the imperial court, ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' (Minister of the Right or "vice-premier") in 1218.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Sanetomo" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', pp. 633–634|page=633}} Eventually, Sanetomo lapsed into inactivity and despair, plagued by fear of assassination and tormented by his chronic [[:en:alcoholism|alcoholism]] (an addiction which Priest [[:en:Eisai|Eisai]] once tried to break by replacing alcohol with [[:en:Tea|Tea]]).{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === Death === [[File:Tsurugaoka.jpg|thumb|Grand stairway at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in Kamakura – the scene of Sanetomo's assassination]] [[File:KamakuraHachimanguTree.jpg|thumb|Shinto symbolism encompasses the girth of the ancient ginkgo tree at the foot of the stairs leading to the upper level of Kamakura's great Hachiman Shrine. The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. (1962). ''Sovereign and Subject''. Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':

[[:en:Emperor Juntoku|Juntoku]] | 1blankname = ''[[:en:Shikken|Shikken]]'' | 1namedata = [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]]
[[:en:Hōjō Yoshitoki|Hōjō Yoshitoki]] | predecessor = [[Minamoto no Yoriie]] | successor = [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]] | birth_date = {{birth date|1192|9|17}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1219|2|13|1192|9|17}} | death_place = | spouse = Bomon Nobuko | children = | father = [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | mother = [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] | signature = Minamoto no Sanetomo kao.jpg | nationality = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | branch = [[Minamoto clan]] }} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Sanetomo'''|源 実朝||September 12, 1192 – February 13, 1219, r. 1203–1219}} was the third ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. He was the second son of the Kamakura shogunate founder, [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. His mother was [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and his older brother was the second Kamakura shogun [[Minamoto no Yoriie]]. His childhood name was {{nihongo|'''Senman'''|千万}}. He was the last head of the [[Minamoto clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was {{nihongo|'''Daijijiden seini'i goshoko jingi'''|大慈寺殿正二位丞相公神儀}}. He was an accomplished ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poet. == Early life == [[File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|thumb|220px|Sanetomo in court clothing, painting by the priest Goshin.]] {{more citations needed|section|date=March 2018}} After the death of Yoritomo in 1199, Sanetomo's maternal grandfather [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]] usurped the political and military power of the Shogunate, relegating the position and title of ''[[:en:Sei-i Taishōgun|Sei-i Taishōgun]]'', or ''shōgun'', to a figurehead. Through hereditary succession, Sanetomo's older brother Yoriie became ''Sei-i Taishōgun'' in 1202, only to be stripped of the title a year later and put under house arrest for plotting against the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]].{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} This was presumably to keep the ''shōgun'' a child and thus needing a regent (''shikken'') to make decisions in his place. Shortly thereafter, in 1203, Sanetomo became head of the Minamoto clan and was appointed ''Sei-i Taishōgun''. A year later, Yoriie was assassinated by the Hōjō. Sanetomo was never more than a puppet for his mother [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and maternal uncle Hōjō Yoshitoki, who used him as a pawn in their war with their father Tokimasa; Tokimasa tried to depose his grandson a number of times, beginning in 1205, causing Sanetomo to fear for his life thereafter.{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === ''Waka'' poet === Sanetomo, understanding his own powerlessness and not wanting to meet the same fate as his elder brother, put his time and energy into writing [[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka poetry]] and gaining posts within the powerless but honorary imperial court. Sanetomo was a talented poet, writing over 700 poems between the age 17 and 22 while he was tutored by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}}. He published his private waka collection ''Kinkai Wakashū'', even having one of his [[waka (poetry)#Tanka|tanka]] included in the anthology ''Ogura [[:en:Hyakunin Isshu|Hyakunin Isshu]]'' ("100 Poems by 100 Poets"), a collection of Japanese poems of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and early Kamakura periods. Sanetomo reached the third-highest post of the imperial court, ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' (Minister of the Right or "vice-premier") in 1218.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Sanetomo" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', pp. 633–634|page=633}} Eventually, Sanetomo lapsed into inactivity and despair, plagued by fear of assassination and tormented by his chronic [[:en:alcoholism|alcoholism]] (an addiction which Priest [[:en:Eisai|Eisai]] once tried to break by replacing alcohol with [[:en:Tea|Tea]]).{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === Death === [[File:Tsurugaoka.jpg|thumb|Grand stairway at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in Kamakura – the scene of Sanetomo's assassination]] [[File:KamakuraHachimanguTree.jpg|thumb|Shinto symbolism encompasses the girth of the ancient ginkgo tree at the foot of the stairs leading to the upper level of Kamakura's great Hachiman Shrine. The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. (1962). ''Sovereign and Subject''. Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':

Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':

[[arz:ميناموتو وسانيتومو]][[be:Мінамота-но Санэтома]][[ca:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[de:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[en:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[es:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[eu:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[fa:میناموتو نو سانه‌تومو]][[fr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[it:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[ja:源実朝]][[ko:미나모토노 사네토모]][[la:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[mr:मिनामोटो नो सानेतोमो]][[nl:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[pl:Sanetomo Minamoto]][[pt:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[ru:Минамото-но Санэтомо]][[sv:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[th:มินาโมโตะ โนะ ซาเนโตโมะ]][[tr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[uk:Мінамото но Санетомо]][[vi:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[zh-classical:源實朝]][[zh-min-nan:Minamoto no Sanetomo]][[zh:源實朝]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1192|9|17}}|death_date={{death date|1219|2|13}}|birth_place={{ill|Nagoe|WD=Q11415640}}|death_place={{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū|WD=Q701403}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|calligrapher|WD=Q3303330}}; {{ill|poet|WD=Q49757}}; {{ill|writer|WD=Q36180}}|title={{ill|shogun|WD=Q131767}}|spouse={{ill|Bōmon Nobuko|WD=Q498600}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800}}|mother={{ill|Hōjō Masako|WD=Q463961}}|relatives={{ill|Jōgyō|WD=Q11634528}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriie|WD=Q431052}}; {{ill|Sanman|WD=Q11355364}}; {{ill|Ōhime|WD=Q11434217}}|family={{ill|Kawachi Genji|WD=Q5360652}}|notable_works={{ill|Kinkai Wakashū|WD=Q11647307}}|native_name=源実朝|resting_place={{ill|Jufuku-ji|WD=Q167087}}; {{ill|Shōchōju-in|WD=Q17219243}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q174249]] Wikidata name: Minamoto no Sanetomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Sanetomo
{{lang|ja|源 実朝}} | image = File:Painting of Minamoto no Sanetomo by Yashima Gakutei, 1825.jpg | caption = Painting of Minamoto no Sanetomo by [[:en:Yashima Gakutei|Yashima Gakutei]]. [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]] circa 1825. | office = ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' | term_start = 1203 | term_end = 1219 | monarch = [[:en:Emperor Tsuchimikado|Tsuchimikado]]
[[:en:Emperor Juntoku|Juntoku]] | 1blankname = ''[[:en:Shikken|Shikken]]'' | 1namedata = [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]]
[[:en:Hōjō Yoshitoki|Hōjō Yoshitoki]] | predecessor = [[Minamoto no Yoriie]] | successor = [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]] | birth_date = {{birth date|1192|9|17}} | birth_place = | death_date = {{death date and age|1219|2|13|1192|9|17}} | death_place = | spouse = Bomon Nobuko | children = | father = [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | mother = [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] | signature = Minamoto no Sanetomo kao.jpg | nationality = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | allegiance = [[Minamoto clan]] | branch = [[Minamoto clan]] }} {{Nihongo|'''Minamoto no Sanetomo'''|源 実朝||September 12, 1192 – February 13, 1219, r. 1203–1219}} was the third ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]]. He was the second son of the Kamakura shogunate founder, [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]]. His mother was [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and his older brother was the second Kamakura shogun [[Minamoto no Yoriie]]. His childhood name was {{nihongo|'''Senman'''|千万}}. He was the last head of the [[Minamoto clan]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. His [[:en:Dharma name|Dharma name]] was {{nihongo|'''Daijijiden seini'i goshoko jingi'''|大慈寺殿正二位丞相公神儀}}. He was an accomplished ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poet. == Early life == [[File:Minamoto no Sanetomo.jpg|thumb|220px|Sanetomo in court clothing, painting by the priest Goshin.]] {{more citations needed|section|date=March 2018}} After the death of Yoritomo in 1199, Sanetomo's maternal grandfather [[:en:Hōjō Tokimasa|Hōjō Tokimasa]] usurped the political and military power of the Shogunate, relegating the position and title of ''[[:en:Sei-i Taishōgun|Sei-i Taishōgun]]'', or ''shōgun'', to a figurehead. Through hereditary succession, Sanetomo's older brother Yoriie became ''Sei-i Taishōgun'' in 1202, only to be stripped of the title a year later and put under house arrest for plotting against the [[:en:Hōjō clan|Hōjō clan]].{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} This was presumably to keep the ''shōgun'' a child and thus needing a regent (''shikken'') to make decisions in his place. Shortly thereafter, in 1203, Sanetomo became head of the Minamoto clan and was appointed ''Sei-i Taishōgun''. A year later, Yoriie was assassinated by the Hōjō. Sanetomo was never more than a puppet for his mother [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] and maternal uncle Hōjō Yoshitoki, who used him as a pawn in their war with their father Tokimasa; Tokimasa tried to depose his grandson a number of times, beginning in 1205, causing Sanetomo to fear for his life thereafter.{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === ''Waka'' poet === Sanetomo, understanding his own powerlessness and not wanting to meet the same fate as his elder brother, put his time and energy into writing [[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka poetry]] and gaining posts within the powerless but honorary imperial court. Sanetomo was a talented poet, writing over 700 poems between the age 17 and 22 while he was tutored by [[:en:Fujiwara no Teika|Fujiwara no Teika]]{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}}. He published his private waka collection ''Kinkai Wakashū'', even having one of his [[waka (poetry)#Tanka|tanka]] included in the anthology ''Ogura [[:en:Hyakunin Isshu|Hyakunin Isshu]]'' ("100 Poems by 100 Poets"), a collection of Japanese poems of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and early Kamakura periods. Sanetomo reached the third-highest post of the imperial court, ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' (Minister of the Right or "vice-premier") in 1218.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Sanetomo" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', pp. 633–634|page=633}} Eventually, Sanetomo lapsed into inactivity and despair, plagued by fear of assassination and tormented by his chronic [[:en:alcoholism|alcoholism]] (an addiction which Priest [[:en:Eisai|Eisai]] once tried to break by replacing alcohol with [[:en:Tea|Tea]]).{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === Death === [[File:Tsurugaoka.jpg|thumb|Grand stairway at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in Kamakura – the scene of Sanetomo's assassination]] [[File:KamakuraHachimanguTree.jpg|thumb|Shinto symbolism encompasses the girth of the ancient ginkgo tree at the foot of the stairs leading to the upper level of Kamakura's great Hachiman Shrine. The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. (1962). ''Sovereign and Subject''. Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':
Minamoto no Sanetomo | before=[[Minamoto no Yoriie]] | after=[[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]] | years=1203–1219}} {{s-end}} {{Shoguns}} {{Authority control}} {{draft categories| [[Category:1219 deaths]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:13th-century shōguns]] [[Category:Kamakura shōguns]] [[Category:Minamoto clan]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Deified Japanese men]] [[Category:Imperial House of Japan]] [[Category:Samurai]] [[Category:People of Kamakura-period Japan]] [[Category:Assassinated Japanese people]] [[Category:Hyakunin Isshu poets]] [[Category:13th-century Japanese calligraphers]] }} [[en:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[arz:ميناموتو وسانيتومو]] [[be:Мінамота-но Санэтома]] [[ca:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[de:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[es:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[eu:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[fa:میناموتو نو سانه‌تومو]] [[fr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[it:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[ja:源実朝]] [[ko:미나모토노 사네토모]] [[la:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[mr:मिनामोटो नो सानेतोमो]] [[nl:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[pl:Sanetomo Minamoto]] [[pt:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[ru:Минамото-но Санэтомо]] [[sv:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[th:มินาโมโตะ โนะ ซาเนโตโมะ]] [[tr:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[uk:Мінамото но Санетомо]] [[vi:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] [[zh:源實朝]] [[zh-classical:源實朝]] [[zh-min-nan:Minamoto no Sanetomo]] {{wikidata link|Q174249}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q174249}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

He published his private waka collection ''Kinkai Wakashū'', even having one of his [[waka (poetry)#Tanka|tanka]] included in the anthology ''Ogura [[:en:Hyakunin Isshu|Hyakunin Isshu]]'' ("100 Poems by 100 Poets"), a collection of Japanese poems of the [[:en:Heian period|Heian]] and early Kamakura periods. Sanetomo reached the third-highest post of the imperial court, ''[[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]'' (Minister of the Right or "vice-premier") in 1218.Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Sanetomo" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', pp. 633–634|page=633}} Eventually, Sanetomo lapsed into inactivity and despair, plagued by fear of assassination and tormented by his chronic [[:en:alcoholism|alcoholism]] (an addiction which Priest [[:en:Eisai|Eisai]] once tried to break by replacing alcohol with [[:en:Tea|Tea]]).{{Citation needed|date= November 2011}} === Death === [[File:Tsurugaoka.jpg|thumb|Grand stairway at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] in Kamakura – the scene of Sanetomo's assassination]] [[File:KamakuraHachimanguTree.jpg|thumb|Shinto symbolism encompasses the girth of the ancient ginkgo tree at the foot of the stairs leading to the upper level of Kamakura's great Hachiman Shrine. The tree, near the spot where Sanetomo was ambushed and killed, was blown down on 10 March 2010.]] [[File:Minamoto-no-Sanetomo's-cenotaph.jpg|thumb|[[:en:Cenotaph|Cenotaph]] honoring Sanetomo in Kamakura's [[:en:Jufuku-ji|Jufuku-ji]]'s cemetery]] Under heavy snow on the evening of February 13, 1219 (''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]] 1, 27th day of the 1st month''), Sanetomo was coming down from the Senior Shrine at [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]] after participating in a ceremony celebrating his nomination to [[:en:Udaijin|Udaijin]]. His nephew and son of deposed Yoriie, [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], came out from beside the stone stairway of the shrine and assassinated him.Azuma Kagami; Mutsu (1995/06: 102-104) For his act, he was himself beheaded a few hours later, thus bringing the [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] line of the Minamoto clan and their rule in Kamakura to a sudden end. Minamoto no Sanetomo was succeeded by [[:en:Kujō Yoritsune|Kujō Yoritsune]], son-in-law of Yoriie, as fourth ''shōgun'' of the Kamakura shogunate. ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]] ==Eras of Sanetomo's ''bakufu''== The years in which Sanetomo was shogun are more specifically identified by more than one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]''. * ''[[:en:Kennin|Kennin]]'' (1201–1204) * ''[[:en:Genkyū|Genkyū]]'' (1204–1206) * ''[[:en:Ken'ei|Ken'ei]]'' (1206–1207) * ''[[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Jōgen (Kamakura period)]]'' (1207–1211) * ''[[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]'' (1211–1213) * ''[[:en:Kempo (era)|Kenpō]]'' (1213–1219) * ''[[:en:Jōkyū|Jōkyū]]'' (1219–1222) ==See also== * ''[[:en:Azuma Kagami|Azuma Kagami]]'' ==Notes== {{reflist}} ==References== * [http://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~hd1t-situ/azuma/121901.html ''Azuma Kagami''], accessed on September 4, 2008; [http://www.archives.go.jp/english/ourholdings/popup/12.html National Archives of Japan 特103-0001, Digitized image of the ''Azumakagami''] {{in lang|ja}} * {{cite book| last1 = Brinkley| first1 = FRank| last2 = Kikuchi| first2 = Dairoku| title = A history of the Japanese people from the earliest times to the end of the Meiji era| url = https://archive.org/details/bub_gb_JlUCAAAAYAAJ| year = 1915| publisher = The Encyclopædia Britannica Co. }} * {{cite book| author = 神谷道倫| title = 深く歩く鎌倉史跡散策上| year = 2006| publisher = かまくら春秋社| isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 }} * {{cite book| author = Jeffrey P. Mass| title = Court and Bakufu in Japan| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=ijyj-9lHNigC| year = 1995| publisher = Stanford University Press| isbn = 978-0-8047-2473-9 }} * {{cite book| author = David Murray |author2=Kentarō Kaneko | title = Japan: Continuing the history to the close of 1905, with the provisions of the treaty of Portsmouth between Russia and Japan, and supplementary chapters| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=iSANAAAAYAAJ&pg=PA504| year = 1906| publisher = Putnam| page = 504 }} * {{cite book| last = Mutsu| first = I.| title = Kamakura: Fact and Legend| year = 1995| publisher = PeriplusEdition| isbn = 978-0-8048-1968-8 }} * [[:en:Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard Arthur Brabazon]]. (1962). ''Sovereign and Subject''. Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. {{OCLC|1014075}} * {{cite book| last1 = Frédéric| first1 = Louis| last2 = Roth| first2 = Käthe| title = Japan enciklopedia| url = https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC| year = 2002| publisher = Harvard University Press| isbn = 978-0-674-01753-5 }} * {{cite book| last = Rin-siyo| first = Siyun-zai| title = Annales des empereurs du Japon| url = https://archive.org/details/niponodaitsiran00ringoog| year = 1834| publisher = Oriental Translation Fund }} ==External links== * [http://digitalgallery.nypl.org/nypldigital/dgkeysearchdetail.cfm?trg=1&strucID=139733&imageID=110031&word=japan&s=1¬word=&d=&c=&f=&lWord=&lField=&sScope=&sLevel=&sLabel=&total=1807&num=372&imgs=12&pNum=&pos=382 New York Public Library Digital Gallery, an early photograph of Shrine steps where Sanetomo was killed.] {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'':
